topic: DeepSeek V3 Cost Comparison vs GPT-5

competitors.title: DeepSeek V3.2 Matches GPT-5 Performance with 90% Lower Training Costs
key_points: DeepSeek V3.2 matches GPT-5 in reasoning benchmarks despite using fewer total training FLOPs
V3.2 achieved 93.1% accuracy on AIME 2025 and 2386 Codeforces rating
DeepSeek Sparse Attention (DSA) reduces computational complexity from O(L²) to O(Lk)
V3.2 Speciale scored 96.0% on AIME 2025 and gold-medal performance on 2025 IMO
Open-source on Hugging Face; Speciale only available via API
Post-training budget exceeded 10% of pre-training costs
competitors.unique_angle: Focuses on training cost efficiency and architectural innovation (DSA) as the key differentiator, not API pricing

competitors.title: DeepSeek claims 'theoretical' profit margins of 545%
key_points: DeepSeek claimed 545% cost profit margin based on theoretical income
If all usage was billed at R1 pricing, daily revenue would be $562,027
GPU leasing cost would be only $87,072 per day
Actual revenue is substantially lower due to free web/app access and discounts
DeepSeek V3 and R1 pricing are dramatically lower than competitors
competitors.unique_angle: Business model profitability angle — shows the massive cost advantage at infrastructure level

competitors.title: DeepSeek releases 'sparse attention' model that cuts API costs in half
key_points: DeepSeek V3.2-Exp introduces DSA (DeepSeek Sparse Attention)
API costs reduced by up to 50% in long-context scenarios
Lightning indexer + fine-grained token selection reduces attention complexity
Open-weight model on Hugging Face enables third-party verification
Inference cost reduction is distinct from training cost savings
competitors.unique_angle: Focuses specifically on inference cost reduction through sparse attention, a unique technical approach

competitors.title: DeepSeek tests 'sparse attention' to slash AI processing costs
key_points: DeepSeek V3.2-Exp achieves fine-grained sparse attention for the first time
Cut API prices by 50% to demonstrate efficiency gains
R1 training cost was only $6 million vs billions for US competitors
Quadratic attention cost is the fundamental bottleneck for long conversations
OpenAI pioneered sparse transformers in 2019 but DeepSeek achieves finer-grained version
competitors.unique_angle: Technical deep-dive into why attention is the bottleneck and how sparse attention solves it, with historical context

competitors.title: DeepSeek R1 vs OpenAI o1: Which One is Faster, Cheaper and Smarter?
key_points: DeepSeek R1 trained on DeepSeek-V3-Base, MIT license for commercial use
Open weight model challenging proprietary systems
Comparison of benchmark performance across reasoning tasks
Cost comparison showing DeepSeek at fraction of OpenAI pricing
competitors.unique_angle: Direct head-to-head comparison format but focused on R1 vs o1, not V3 vs GPT-5

stats.stat: DeepSeek V3.2 API: $0.28 per 1M input tokens (cache miss), $0.028 per 1M input tokens (cache hit), $0.42 per 1M output tokens
GPT-5.4 API: $2.50 per 1M input tokens, $15.00 per 1M output tokens (xhigh reasoning mode)
DeepSeek V3.2 blended price: $0.32 per 1M tokens vs GPT-5.4 (xhigh) blended price: $5.63 per 1M tokens — DeepSeek is 17.6x cheaper
DeepSeek V3.2 Intelligence Index: 42 (non-reasoning) vs GPT-5.4 Intelligence Index: 57 (xhigh reasoning) — GPT-5.4 scores 35% higher on intelligence
DeepSeek V3.2 cost to evaluate on Intelligence Index: $103.16 vs GPT-5.4 cost: $2,851.01 — GPT-5.4 costs 27.6x more for equivalent evaluation
DeepSeek R1 training cost estimated at $6 million — a fraction of OpenAI's billions in spending
DeepSeek theoretical profit margin: 545% — theoretical daily revenue $562,027 vs GPU leasing cost $87,072
DeepSeek V3.2 achieved 93.1% on AIME 2025 and 73.1% on SWE-Verified, placing it competitive with GPT-5 on reasoning benchmarks
GPT-5.4 Pro: $67.50 blended per 1M tokens — the most expensive OpenAI model
DeepSeek V3.2 output speed: 32 tokens/s vs GPT-5.4 output speed: 79 tokens/s — GPT-5.4 is 2.5x faster
DeepSeek V3.2: 685B total parameters, 37B active parameters, MIT license, 128K context
DeepSeek R1 0528 blended price: $2.36 per 1M tokens — reasoning model cheaper than GPT-5.4 at $5.63

angles.angle: Price-per-Intelligence Breakdown: True Cost of Quality
angles.reason: No competitor article calculates cost-per-intelligence-point ratio. DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.32 blended for Intelligence Index 42 vs GPT-5.4 at $5.63 for Index 57 — the cost-efficiency ratio tells a different story than raw benchmarks

angles.angle: Developer Workflow Cost Calculator: Real-World API Spend
angles.reason: Audience is developers/coders — calculate actual monthly spend for common coding workflows (code review, debugging, documentation) with each model

angles.angle: The Hidden Cost of Verbosity: Token Efficiency Matters
angles.reason: DeepSeek V3.2 generated 15M tokens vs GPT-5.4's 120M tokens on the Intelligence Index. Verbose models cost more per query — this is never discussed in competitor articles

angles.angle: Speed vs Cost Tradeoff: When to Choose Which
angles.reason: GPT-5.4 is 2.5x faster (79 vs 32 tokens/s) but 17.6x more expensive. For batch jobs, DeepSeek wins; for interactive use, speed matters. No one has written this decision framework

angles.angle: Cache Hit Pricing: DeepSeek's Secret Weapon
angles.reason: DeepSeek's $0.028/1M input token cache hit pricing is 10x cheaper than cache miss. This is unique to DeepSeek and not covered by competitors — has major implications for repeated prompt patterns in coding

angles.angle: Data Sovereignty vs Cost: The Enterprise Dilemma
angles.reason: Recent revelations of DeepSeek sharing data with Chinese intelligence create a tradeoff that enterprises must navigate. Combine cost analysis with security/risk assessment
